name: CW Finals Topic 1A - World of Region & Global Divide

questions.questionText: The path that Globalization has paved for the world has increasingly brought nation states together, but it can also be argued that the same phenomenon also necessitated that countries come up with regional groupings in order to equip themselves better with the demands and odds brought forth by Globalization. There is truism in the saying that Unity is attained amidst diversity.

In World of Regions, the path that _____________ has paved for the world has increasingly brought nation states together, but it can also be argued that the same phenomenon also necessitated that countries come up with regional groupings in order to equip themselves better with the demands and odds brought forth by Globalization. There is truism in the saying that Unity is attained amidst diversity.

The desire to be a part of one is a product of a process, a well-defined system that came to emerge as a by-product of time, situation and various factors that may be categorized as economic, political, historical and cultural that led to the amalgamation of countries and even societies.

Regionalism and the desire to be a part of one is a product of a process, a well-defined system that came to emerge as a by-product of time, situation and various factors that may be categorized as _________, _________, _________, and _________ that led to the amalgamation of countries and even societies.
UNDP calculates the HDI based on three core dimensions, what are these 3 dimensions?
questions.answer: Long and Healthy Life, Access to Knowledge, Decent Standard of Living

questions.questionText: He stated that: In effect, this is not a strict geographical categorization of the world but one based on economic inequalities which happen to have some cartographic coherence. It also emphasizes that both North and South are, together, drawn into global processes rather than existing as separate slices of the world.
Are countries where many people may have limited access to basic services like healthcare, education, and stable jobs.
The countries in the south may be considered LEDCs due to a number of reasons, among them including an ______________________ and a __________________.

He stated that because of past colonialism and ongoing economic dependence, the global system is set up in a way that helps rich countries become even richer, while making it harder for poorer countries to grow and improve their situation.

name: CW Finals Topic 1B - First, Second, & Third World

questions.questionText: A term that first emerged in 1996. It is a dichotomy between the poor and the wealthy, which are not necessarily, but predominantly around their corresponding geographical locations (north and south).

A French scholar by the name of ________________ coined the term “Third World” as a term to distinguish the formerly colonized and noncolonized societies of Asia, Africa and Latin America from the modernized “first” world capitalism and the modernizing “second” world of socialism.

A _________________ by the name of Alfred Sauvy coined the term “Third World” as a term to distinguish the formerly colonized and noncolonized societies of Asia, Africa and Latin America from the modernized “first” world capitalism and the modernizing “second” world of socialism.

Before the end of cold war, First, Second, and Third World has different meaning, and these are the:

First World - ________ Countries
Second World - ________ Countries
Third World - Countries that are ___________.

In 1983, former German Councilor _____________ published “The Brandt Report”. This report identified as North/South line. this map differentiates the South being “The Rich North” while the other is “The Poor South”.

In 1983, former German Councilor Willy Brandt published “_________________”. This report identified as North/South line. this map differentiates the South being “The Rich North” while the other is “The Poor South”.

name: CW Finals Topic 1C - Flashpoints and Perspective of the Divide

questions.questionText: As underdeveloped countries become more visible, they are often grouped under the term “_______________”.
A few decades ago, the South was associated with?
questions.answer: Starvation, Malnutrition, Poverty, Epidemics, Low Educational Levels, Political Authoritarianism and Dictatorships

questions.questionText: According to _________________, inequality and inequity are core features of the North–South divide. Inequality is not just a simple problem that can be solved by giving money. It is a deep structural issue that limits the potential of individuals and societies. It also influences power relationships within countries and between nations
According to __________________, the term Global South is a historic and decontextualized concept.
It omits a critical core of dynamic variables that characterize different kinds of countries, especially __________, __________, __________, __________, and __________ variables.
According to him, 80% of the world's middle-class population will be living in developing countries by 2030.
As opined by _________________, there is this ongoing global transformation is a phenomenon known as "South-South cooperation".
In fact, _______________ enumerated the three factors that direct the economic development of states within the Global South.

Jean Grugel enumerated 3 factors that direct the economic development of states within the Global South, these are:

1. ________________ within and between nation states.
2. Integration and cooperation within ______________________.
3. The resulting position of states and regions within the _________________ and related ______________________________.
questions.answer: Elite Behaviors, Geographic Area, Global Market, Political Economic Hierarchy

questions.questionText: As he defined it as a "compression of the world", the improvement in economic interdependence will contribute to the further "shrinking" of the world and is expected to shake up even more the volatile Global North - South divide.

Closing or mitigating the divide has been a goal for many developmental initiatives. The United Nations has developed a program dedicated to narrowing the divide through its Millennium Development Goals aimed at Sustainable development. This includes improving education and health care, promoting gender equality, and ensuring environmental sustainability. (True or False).

Closing or mitigating the divide has been a goal for many developmental initiatives. The International Monetary Fund has developed a program dedicated to narrowing the divide through its Millennium Development Goals aimed at Sustainable development. This includes improving education and health care, promoting gender equality, and ensuring environmental sustainability. (True or False).

In addition, the IMF, World Bank and other progressive Northern countries have embarked on developmental initiatives such as providing loans and grants to the Global south countries in order that these nation states may also attain development in the long run. (True or False).

In addition, the IDA, OPEC, and other progressive NATO countries have embarked on developmental initiatives such as providing loans and grants to the Global south countries in order that these nation states may also attain development in the long run. (True or False).
Member countries of BRICS.
questions.answer: Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa

questions.questionText: The term Global South removes the negative political and economic labeling previously associated with Third World. (True or False).
The term Global North removes the negative political and economic labeling previously associated with Third World. (True or False).
The term Global South is normally used to refer to countries faced with social, political, and economic challenges. (True or False).
The term Global North is normally used to refer to countries faced with social, political, and economic challenges. (True or False).
Even with improvements in socio-economic conditions and increased wealth in some Global South countries, major differences still exist between the North and South. (True or False).

name: CW Finals Topic 2B - The Imperative for Regionalism in Asia

questions.questionText: Known for being open and flexible, unlike the protectionist trade blocs formed during the 1930s Great Depression, which restricted trade and worsened the global economic downturn. Learning from that failure, the post-war system (like GATT, later the WTO) emphasized nondiscrimination in trade. Although some experts worry regionalism could harm global trade system.

_______, ______________, and the integration of "real" ____________ activity.

Asia’s growing economic interdependence provides many opportunities for cooperation. These are divided into four major areas in this analysis, the second one is:

___________ integration.

_______________________ policy links.

Asia’s growing economic interdependence provides many opportunities for cooperation. These are divided into four major areas in this analysis, the fourth one is:

Shared _________ and ________________ concerns.

name: CW Finals Topic 2A - Asian Regionalism & Globalization of Religion

questions.questionText: Refers to a manifestation of shared cultural identity and purpose. It involves creating institutions that allow countries within a specific geographical area to take collective action.
He argues regional organizations adapt global norms to local contexts, showing regionalism as a mix of external influence and local agency

He argues that regional organizations are shaped not only by formal rules but also by distinctive regional norms and practices, which guide how members interact, confer legitimacy, and define the region’s identity.
He explains that regionalism has evolved from economic cooperation into broader political and social ties, with its form varying by historical and local context rather than being uniform
Characteristics of Regionalism.
questions.answer: Institutional Arrangements, Policy Coordination, Stages of Integration

questions.questionText: What are the factors when the gravity of world economic, political, and security affairs when moved to Asia?
Unlike the historical trade blocs of the 1930s, which were protectionist and contributed to economic collapse, modern Asian regionalism is characterized by its openness and accommodating nature.

Unlike the historical trade blocs of the 1930s, which were ______________ and contributed to economic collapse, modern Asian regionalism is characterized by its _____________ and _________________ nature.
He coined the term "Yuxi Circle".
A regional organization made up of Southeast Asian countries. It was created on August 8, 1967 in Bangkok Thailand.
When was ASEAN created?

One of the 7 aims and purposes of ASEAN states that:

Accelerate _______________, ________________, _________________ through equality and partnership to build a peaceful and prosperous Southeast Asia.
questions.answer: Economic Growth, Social Progress, Cultural Development

questions.questionText:
One of the 7 aims and purposes of ASEAN states that:

Promote _____________________ and _________ through respect for justice, rule of law, and principles of the UN charter.

One of the 7 aims and purposes of ASEAN states that:

Promote ________________________ and ____________________ in economic, social, cultural, technical, scientific, and administrative fields.
